feedback.text: Dear EU Commission, I support the Stop Killing Games initiative and urge the Digital Fairness Act to protect video game ownership. Purchased digital products should remain accessible indefinitely, without planned obsolescence. Games should be developed for offline play, LAN/direct connect (peer to peer) play or provide tools for community-run servers. Companies should not retroactively censor or alter game content, such as story, gameplay elements or mature themes, or if they choose to do so, both original and modified versions should be available. Thank you for addressing this.
